title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXXVI. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old woman in Surrey,
        Who, was morn, noon, and night in a hurry;
                Call'd her husband a fool,
                Drove the children to school,
        The worrying old woman of Surrey.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCLXXVI. Gaffers And Gammers." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
